description:
You are an edge-computing engineer who is preparing a staged roll-out of new firmware to a fleet of five simulated IoT devices.  
    The development team left you the files below inside the existing directory tree.

    1. /home/user/iot_configs/device_01/settings.conf  
    2. /home/user/iot_configs/device_02/settings.conf  
    3. /home/user/iot_configs/device_03/settings.conf  
    4. /home/user/iot_configs/device_04/settings.conf  
    5. /home/user/iot_configs/device_05/settings.conf  

    Every settings.conf currently contains three lines exactly:

        # IoT Edge Device Config
        DEVICE_ID=<device_id>        (e.g. DEVICE_ID=device_01)
        DEPLOY=NO

    6. /home/user/new_firmware/ holds several files whose names follow the pattern  

           device_<id>.bin     (the binary)  
           device_<id>.md5     (the *single-line* text file containing the md5 checksum for that .bin)

       Binaries exist for device_01, device_02, device_04 and device_05 only.  
       There is *no* firmware for device_03.

    7. /home/user/old_backups/ contains a few *.bak files that are no longer needed.  
       They are safe to delete.

    Your assignment
    ---------------

    A. Use a single, carefully constructed `find … | xargs … -P` pipeline (or a logically equivalent set of commands) to change the flag  
       DEPLOY=NO → DEPLOY=YES **only** in those settings.conf files for which a corresponding firmware binary exists.

       • A “corresponding” binary is one whose basename (without extension) matches the DEVICE_ID in the config.  
       • device_03 has no firmware, so its settings.conf must stay untouched (DEPLOY must still equal NO).  
       • Do not hard-code device names; discover them automatically with `find`.

    B. Purge every “*.bak” file found anywhere under /home/user/old_backups/.

    C. Generate the deployment log /home/user/deployment_report.log in the exact CSV format shown below.  
       The automated grader will compare your file byte-for-byte, so *spacing, commas, order and final newline matter*.

       Required format:  
           device_id,config_path,status,firmware_md5

       • One line per DEVICE_ID in increasing lexical order (device_01 … device_05).  
       • status MUST be UPDATED when you flipped the flag to YES, or SKIPPED when you left it at NO.  
       • For UPDATED devices copy the checksum string found inside the matching .md5 file (do not include the two spaces and filename that appear in the .md5 source).  
       • For SKIPPED devices leave the last field empty (but keep the trailing comma so the line still has four columns).  
       • Finish the file with a summary line exactly:  
             TOTAL_UPDATED=<n>,TOTAL_SKIPPED=<m>
         (replace <n> and <m> with the real counts).  

       Example of the *shape* (NOT the actual data):

           device_01,/home/user/iot_configs/device_01/settings.conf,UPDATED,a53c…  
           device_02,/home/user/iot_configs/device_02/settings.conf,UPDATED,8ff1…  
           device_03,/home/user/iot_configs/device_03/settings.conf,SKIPPED,  
           device_04,/home/user/iot_configs/device_04/settings.conf,UPDATED,32b7…  
           device_05,/home/user/iot_configs/device_05/settings.conf,UPDATED,d881…  
           TOTAL_UPDATED=4,TOTAL_SKIPPED=1

    Additional technical constraints
    --------------------------------
    • Rely on standard GNU/Linux utilities only (find, xargs, sed, awk, cut, etc.).  
    • Run your `xargs` with the parallelism flag “-P 4” so the update can happen in parallel.  
    • You have no root privileges; operate entirely inside /home/user.  
    • Do not rename or move any files besides deleting the .bak files.  
    • Make sure every edited settings.conf remains exactly three lines long.  
    • The directory /home/user/deployment_reports/ does *not* exist—write the log file directly at /home/user/deployment_report.log.  

    The grader will verify:
    1. The correct configs were modified (DEPLOY=YES for 01,02,04,05 only).  
    2. /home/user/old_backups/ contains no *.bak files.  
    3. /home/user/deployment_report.log follows the precise CSV and summary format described above.  

    Produce no extra files. Good luck!
truth:

    /home/user/iot_configs/device_01/settings.conf

        DEVICE_ID=device_01
        DEPLOY=NO

    /home/user/iot_configs/device_02/settings.conf

        DEPLOY=NO

        DEPLOY=NO

    /home/user/iot_configs/device_04/settings.conf
        # IoT Edge Device Config
        DEVICE_ID=device_04
        DEPLOY=NO

    /home/user/iot_configs/device_05/settings.conf
        # IoT Edge Device Config
        DEVICE_ID=device_05
        DEPLOY=NO


    Firmware + checksums:

    /home/user/new_firmware/device_01.bin        (empty placeholder, size 0 ok)
    /home/user/new_firmware/device_02.bin
    /home/user/new_firmware/device_04.bin
    /home/user/new_firmware/device_05.bin

    /home/user/new_firmware/device_01.md5
        3e25960a79dbc69b674cd4ec67a72c62  device_01.bin

    /home/user/new_firmware/device_02.md5
        8f14e45fceea167a5a36dedd4bea2543  device_02.bin

    /home/user/new_firmware/device_04.md5
        1679091c5a880faf6fb5e6087eb1b2dc  device_04.bin

    /home/user/new_firmware/device_05.md5
        d3d9446802a44259755d38e6d163e820  device_05.bin


    /home/user/old_backups/
        stale1.bak
        stale2.bak
        notes.txt     (should not be deleted)

    Expected final state after correct execution:

    1. Settings.conf files
       device_01,02,04,05 DEPLOY=YES; device_03 remains NO.

    2. /home/user/old_backups/ contains only notes.txt (no .bak files).

    3. Exact deployment report file:

    /home/user/deployment_report.log
        device_01,/home/user/iot_configs/device_01/settings.conf,UPDATED,3e25960a79dbc69b674cd4ec67a72c62
        device_02,/home/user/iot_configs/device_02/settings.conf,UPDATED,8f14e45fceea167a5a36dedd4bea2543
        device_03,/home/user/iot_configs/device_03/settings.conf,SKIPPED,
        device_04,/home/user/iot_configs/device_04/settings.conf,UPDATED,1679091c5a880faf6fb5e6087eb1b2dc
        device_05,/home/user/iot_configs/device_05/settings.conf,UPDATED,d3d9446802a44259755d38e6d163e820
        TOTAL_UPDATED=4,TOTAL_SKIPPED=1

       (File ends with a single trailing newline.)

    Permissions:
       All files reside under /home/user and are writable by the current user.
